questions.stem: The book ______ by many students in our school last year.
questions.explanation: 本题考查一般过去时的被动语态。由时间状语last year可知动作发生在过去，且book与read是被动关系（书被读），故用was/were+过去分词结构。主语the book是单数，因此选C was read。A是主动语态第三人称单数；B是一般现在时被动语态，时态不符；D是主动语态过去式，不能表示被动。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—______ you ______ the film Frozen II?
—Yes, I ______ it with my sister last weekend.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。问句强调过去动作对现在的影响（看过这部电影吗？），用现在完成时Have you seen；答句有明确过去时间状语last weekend，用一般过去时saw。A项问句用一般过去时，只强调动作发生，不强调对现在的影响；C项时态搭配错误；D项答句用现在完成时，与last weekend矛盾。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is standing under the tree is my best friend.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词the boy指人，且关系代词在从句中作主语（is standing），因此用who。which指物；whose表示所属关系（...的），在从句中作定语；whom指人但只能作宾语。解题口诀：指人用who/whom/that，指物用which/that，缺主语用who/which/that，缺宾语可用whom/who/which/that或不填。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: My father ______ in this factory since he left school, but he ______ there any more because it closed last month.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时/现在时的综合运用。第一空有since引导的时间状语从句（since he left school），主句用现在完成时has worked，表示从过去持续到现在的动作；第二空根据any more（不再）和closed last month（上个月倒闭），表示现在的状态（现在不在那里工作了），用一般现在时doesn't work。A项第二空didn't work只表示过去不工作，不表示现在状态；B项第一空worked不能体现从过去持续到现在的含义；D项第二空hasn't worked表示从过去到现在都不工作，与closed last month的时间点衔接不如一般现在时准确。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The weather is so hot that I need something to ______ my thirst.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词词义辨析及固定搭配。quench thirst是固定搭配，意为'解渴'。solve意为'解决（问题）'；increase意为'增加'，与语境相反；discover意为'发现'。根据语境'天气很热'，可知需要'解渴'，故选B。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should ______ old clothes instead of throwing them away.
questions.explanation: 本题考查give短语辨析。give away意为'捐赠，赠送'；give up意为'放弃'；give in意为'屈服，让步'；give out意为'分发，耗尽'。根据语境'instead of throwing them away（而不是扔掉）'，可知应该'捐赠'旧衣服，故选A。记忆技巧：away表示'离开'，把东西给出去让别人拿走，即'捐赠'。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: It is ______ to protect the environment. We should take action immediately.
questions.explanation: 本题考查词性转换。句型为It is + 形容词 + to do sth.（做某事是...的），因此需要形容词necessary（必要的）。B是副词；C是名词；D是动词。根据句子结构，系动词is后接形容词作表语，故选A。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a special project called "Green Campus". The purpose was to make students understand the (8)______ of protecting the environment.

Every class was asked to design a creative recycling bin. My classmates and I decided to make one that looked like a cute panda. We used old boxes, plastic bottles and waste paper (9)______ it. At first, we found it difficult to make the panda's ears stand up. (10)______, after several tries, we finally succeeded.

When we placed the bin in the hallway, many students stopped to look at it. Some even took photos with it. To our (11)______, more students started putting rubbish into the right bins. The project not only improved our creativity but also raised everyone's awareness of environmental protection.

Now, the "Green Campus" project has become very popular in our school. I believe small actions can make a big (12)______ to our planet.
questions.explanation: 本题考查词性辨析及语境理解。定冠词the后应接名词，the importance of...意为'...的重要性'。A是形容词；C是副词；D是动词（进口）或名词（进口商品），不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a special project called "Green Campus". The purpose was to make students understand the (8)______ of protecting the environment.

Every class was asked to design a creative recycling bin. My classmates and I decided to make one that looked like a cute panda. We used old boxes, plastic bottles and waste paper (9)______ it. At first, we found it difficult to make the panda's ears stand up. (10)______, after several tries, we finally succeeded.

When we placed the bin in the hallway, many students stopped to look at it. Some even took photos with it. To our (11)______, more students started putting rubbish into the right bins. The project not only improved our creativity but also raised everyone's awareness of environmental protection.

Now, the "Green Campus" project has become very popular in our school. I believe small actions can make a big (12)______ to our planet.
questions.explanation: 本题考查非谓语动词固定搭配。use sth. to do sth.（用某物做某事）是固定结构，此处用不定式to build表示目的。A是动词原形，不能直接接在宾语后；B是现在分词，表示主动或进行；D是过去式/过去分词，不符合use的用法结构。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a special project called "Green Campus". The purpose was to make students understand the (8)______ of protecting the environment.

Every class was asked to design a creative recycling bin. My classmates and I decided to make one that looked like a cute panda. We used old boxes, plastic bottles and waste paper (9)______ it. At first, we found it difficult to make the panda's ears stand up. (10)______, after several tries, we finally succeeded.

When we placed the bin in the hallway, many students stopped to look at it. Some even took photos with it. To our (11)______, more students started putting rubbish into the right bins. The project not only improved our creativity but also raised everyone's awareness of environmental protection.

Now, the "Green Campus" project has become very popular in our school. I believe small actions can make a big (12)______ to our planet.
questions.explanation: 本题考查逻辑关系。前句说'我们发现很难让熊猫耳朵立起来'，后句说'经过几次尝试后终于成功了'，前后是转折关系，因此用However（然而）。Therefore表示因果（因此）；Besides表示递进（此外）；Otherwise表示假设（否则）。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a special project called "Green Campus". The purpose was to make students understand the (8)______ of protecting the environment.

Every class was asked to design a creative recycling bin. My classmates and I decided to make one that looked like a cute panda. We used old boxes, plastic bottles and waste paper (9)______ it. At first, we found it difficult to make the panda's ears stand up. (10)______, after several tries, we finally succeeded.

When we placed the bin in the hallway, many students stopped to look at it. Some even took photos with it. To our (11)______, more students started putting rubbish into the right bins. The project not only improved our creativity but also raised everyone's awareness of environmental protection.

Now, the "Green Campus" project has become very popular in our school. I believe small actions can make a big (12)______ to our planet.
questions.explanation: 本题考查固定搭配。to one's surprise是固定短语，意为'令某人惊讶的是'，其中surprise是名词。A是形容词（感到惊讶的）；C是形容词（令人惊讶的）；D是动词第三人称单数或名词复数。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a special project called "Green Campus". The purpose was to make students understand the (8)______ of protecting the environment.

Every class was asked to design a creative recycling bin. My classmates and I decided to make one that looked like a cute panda. We used old boxes, plastic bottles and waste paper (9)______ it. At first, we found it difficult to make the panda's ears stand up. (10)______, after several tries, we finally succeeded.

When we placed the bin in the hallway, many students stopped to look at it. Some even took photos with it. To our (11)______, more students started putting rubbish into the right bins. The project not only improved our creativity but also raised everyone's awareness of environmental protection.

Now, the "Green Campus" project has become very popular in our school. I believe small actions can make a big (12)______ to our planet.
questions.explanation: 本题考查固定搭配。make a difference是固定短语，意为'有影响，起作用'。make a decision意为'做决定'；make progress意为'取得进步'（progress不可数，不加a）；make a mistake意为'犯错误'。根据语境'小行动能对地球产生大影响'，选C。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Do you know how to read faster and remember more? Many students complain that they forget what they read quickly. Here are some scientific ways to improve your reading skills.

First, try the "pointer method". Use your finger or a pen to guide your eyes while reading. This helps you avoid going back to reread words, which slows you down by 30%.

Second, don't read word by word. Instead, try to read groups of words together. Your brain can understand information better when you see meaningful phrases rather than single words.

Third, take short breaks. After reading for 25 minutes, rest for 5 minutes. Research shows that our brains need time to process information. Without breaks, you may feel tired and lose concentration.

Finally, ask yourself questions after reading each paragraph. This active reading method helps you stay focused and improves your understanding by 40%, according to a study from Harvard University.

Practice these methods for three weeks, and you will see the difference.
questions.stem: According to the passage, how much can the pointer method reduce rereading?
questions.explanation: 本题为细节理解题。根据第二段最后一句'...which slows you down by 30%'（这会让你慢30%），可知指读法能避免回读，从而节省30%的时间，即减少30%的回读。A、B数字错误；D是最后一段提到的主动阅读法提高理解力的比例。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
Do you know how to read faster and remember more? Many students complain that they forget what they read quickly. Here are some scientific ways to improve your reading skills.

First, try the "pointer method". Use your finger or a pen to guide your eyes while reading. This helps you avoid going back to reread words, which slows you down by 30%.

Second, don't read word by word. Instead, try to read groups of words together. Your brain can understand information better when you see meaningful phrases rather than single words.

Third, take short breaks. After reading for 25 minutes, rest for 5 minutes. Research shows that our brains need time to process information. Without breaks, you may feel tired and lose concentration.

Finally, ask yourself questions after reading each paragraph. This active reading method helps you stay focused and improves your understanding by 40%, according to a study from Harvard University.

Practice these methods for three weeks, and you will see the difference.
questions.stem: What does the underlined word "concentration" mean in Paragraph 3?
questions.explanation: 本题为词义猜测题。根据上下文'Without breaks, you may feel tired and lose concentration'（不休息，你可能会感到疲劳并失去concentration），结合语境，疲劳时失去的是'注意力'，因此选B。concentration由动词concentrate（集中）变化而来，词根centr表示'中心'，con-表示'一起'，字面意思'一起到中心'，即'集中'。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
Do you know how to read faster and remember more? Many students complain that they forget what they read quickly. Here are some scientific ways to improve your reading skills.

First, try the "pointer method". Use your finger or a pen to guide your eyes while reading. This helps you avoid going back to reread words, which slows you down by 30%.

Second, don't read word by word. Instead, try to read groups of words together. Your brain can understand information better when you see meaningful phrases rather than single words.

Third, take short breaks. After reading for 25 minutes, rest for 5 minutes. Research shows that our brains need time to process information. Without breaks, you may feel tired and lose concentration.

Finally, ask yourself questions after reading each paragraph. This active reading method helps you stay focused and improves your understanding by 40%, according to a study from Harvard University.

Practice these methods for three weeks, and you will see the difference.
questions.stem: What can we learn from the passage?
options: A. We should read for hours without stopping.
B. Reading single words is faster than reading phrases.
C. Active reading helps improve understanding.
D. Harvard University invented the pointer method.
questions.explanation: 本题为推理判断题。根据最后一段'This active reading method helps you stay focused and improves your understanding by 40%'（这种主动阅读法帮助你集中注意力并提高40%的理解力），可推断出主动阅读有助于提高理解力，故选C。A与第三段建议休息相反；B与第二段'不要逐字读，要读词组'相反；D错误，Harvard只是做了关于主动阅读的研究，并未发明指读法。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
Do you know how to read faster and remember more? Many students complain that they forget what they read quickly. Here are some scientific ways to improve your reading skills.

First, try the "pointer method". Use your finger or a pen to guide your eyes while reading. This helps you avoid going back to reread words, which slows you down by 30%.

Second, don't read word by word. Instead, try to read groups of words together. Your brain can understand information better when you see meaningful phrases rather than single words.

Third, take short breaks. After reading for 25 minutes, rest for 5 minutes. Research shows that our brains need time to process information. Without breaks, you may feel tired and lose concentration.

Finally, ask yourself questions after reading each paragraph. This active reading method helps you stay focused and improves your understanding by 40%, according to a study from Harvard University.

Practice these methods for three weeks, and you will see the difference.
questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: B. How to Read Faster and Better
questions.explanation: 本题为主旨大意题。文章首段提出'如何读得更快记得更多'的问题，随后分四点介绍提高阅读速度和理解力的科学方法，因此B项'如何更快更好地阅读'最能概括全文。A项'阅读的历史'文中未提；C项'阅读中的问题'只在开头提及，不是重点；D项'哈佛阅读研究'只是细节，不能概括全文。选标题技巧：要涵盖主要内容，既不能太宽泛也不能太狭窄，通常关注首尾段。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Many students feel stressed because they have too much homework and too little time. However, good time management can help solve this problem. (17)______

First, make a to-do list every morning. Write down all the tasks you need to do and put the most important ones at the top. (18)______

Second, avoid distractions while studying. Turn off your phone and find a quiet place. If you focus on your work, you will finish it faster. (19)______

Finally, don't forget to take breaks. Your brain needs rest to work well. After studying for an hour, spend ten minutes walking or listening to music. (20)______

By following these tips, you can manage your time better and enjoy your school life more.
options: A. This helps you organize your day clearly.
B. However, playing games is not a good choice for breaks.
C. Here are some useful tips for managing your time.
D. Then you will have more free time for hobbies.
E. Many students spend hours on homework but achieve little.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接。第一段提出'时间管理能解决问题'，接下来几段分别介绍具体方法，因此此处需要一句承上启下的过渡句，引出下文的建议。C项'这里有一些管理时间的有用建议'符合逻辑。A指代不明；B是转折，但此处不需要转折；D是结果，应放在结尾；E是问题描述，与下文解决方法重复。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many students feel stressed because they have too much homework and too little time. However, good time management can help solve this problem. (17)______

First, make a to-do list every morning. Write down all the tasks you need to do and put the most important ones at the top. (18)______

Second, avoid distractions while studying. Turn off your phone and find a quiet place. If you focus on your work, you will finish it faster. (19)______

Finally, don't forget to take breaks. Your brain needs rest to work well. After studying for an hour, spend ten minutes walking or listening to music. (20)______

By following these tips, you can manage your time better and enjoy your school life more.
options: A. This helps you organize your day clearly.
B. However, playing games is not a good choice for breaks.
C. Here are some useful tips for managing your time.
D. Then you will have more free time for hobbies.
E. Many students spend hours on homework but achieve little.
questions.explanation: 本题考查指代关系。前句讲'列待办清单，把重要任务放顶部'，A项'这帮助你清晰地安排你的一天'中This指代前文的make a to-do list，逻辑连贯。其他选项：B讲休息，与此段主题不符；C是总起句，已用过；D是结果，应放在段尾或文章结尾；E是问题，与上下文无关。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many students feel stressed because they have too much homework and too little time. However, good time management can help solve this problem. (17)______

First, make a to-do list every morning. Write down all the tasks you need to do and put the most important ones at the top. (18)______

Second, avoid distractions while studying. Turn off your phone and find a quiet place. If you focus on your work, you will finish it faster. (19)______

Finally, don't forget to take breaks. Your brain needs rest to work well. After studying for an hour, spend ten minutes walking or listening to music. (20)______

By following these tips, you can manage your time better and enjoy your school life more.
options: A. This helps you organize your day clearly.
B. However, playing games is not a good choice for breaks.
C. Here are some useful tips for managing your time.
D. Then you will have more free time for hobbies.
E. Many students spend hours on homework but achieve little.
questions.explanation: 本题考查因果关系。前句讲'如果专注学习，你会更快完成作业'，D项'然后你就有更多空闲时间用于爱好'是专注学习带来的结果，then表示时间/因果顺承，符合逻辑。A已被选用；B是转折，与此处顺承关系不符；C是总起句；E是问题描述。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many students feel stressed because they have too much homework and too little time. However, good time management can help solve this problem. (17)______

First, make a to-do list every morning. Write down all the tasks you need to do and put the most important ones at the top. (18)______

Second, avoid distractions while studying. Turn off your phone and find a quiet place. If you focus on your work, you will finish it faster. (19)______

Finally, don't forget to take breaks. Your brain needs rest to work well. After studying for an hour, spend ten minutes walking or listening to music. (20)______

By following these tips, you can manage your time better and enjoy your school life more.
options: A. This helps you organize your day clearly.
B. However, playing games is not a good choice for breaks.
C. Here are some useful tips for managing your time.
D. Then you will have more free time for hobbies.
E. Many students spend hours on homework but achieve little.
questions.explanation: 本题考查转折关系。本段主题是休息，前句说'散步或听音乐'，B项'然而，玩游戏不是休息的好选择'用However转折，补充说明什么是不恰当的休息方式，使建议更完整。A、C、D均已被选用或不符合语境。
questions.label: 短文填空
